concept: The machine drew the hidden edge. A machine-learning model combed years of Alaska seismic recordings and IGNITED about 1,750 uncatalogued earthquakes out of the noise under its scan. They were not scattered. They fell into one 250-kilometer line, the razor edge of the Yakutat microplate grinding under Alaska. We watch the edge appear under the scan, become a line, become a diving slab, loom toward the Denali Fault that broke in 2002, and then hit its honest limit.
honest_caveat: The model shows WHERE the edge sits, drawn from past earthquakes. It cannot tell you WHEN it moves. Knowing where it is makes hazard maps more honest; it is not a forecast. The Denali stress link is a hypothesis the researchers PROPOSE, drawn as a ghosted vector, never a confident causal arrow.
references: NYT/Reuters editorial data-map clarity for the fault line + labels

fingerprint.palette: basalt and obsidian ground, an incandescent crimson-to-orange seismic line, bone-white station pins, a faint slate graticule
fingerprint.metaphor: the machine draws the hidden edge — a buried plate's razor line pulled out of a decade of seismic noise

palette: basalt and obsidian ground, an incandescent crimson-to-orange seismic line, bone-white station pins, a faint slate graticule
divergence_note: Opposite to the last three on every structural axis. The beluga was an eye-level naturalistic creature portrait; the permafrost was a side-on thermal CROSS-SECTION descending vertically; the sonar was an amber INSTRUMENT SCREEN of a fish-count. This one opens on a TOP-DOWN scan of Alaska's ground where a hidden fault edge IGNITES out of noise and extends horizontally across 250 km, structure-as-subject in a basalt + incandescent-crimson world with bone-white pins, then travels through genuinely different worlds (map, diving slab, a low-angle looming fault, a drawn limit). Different POV, motion, hero, layout, and color world; the metaphor is a machine revealing a buried edge, not a portrait, a cutaway, or a counting readout.

beats.new: PREMISE IN SECOND ONE: a machine SCAN-LINE sweeps a top-down dark field of Alaska's ground and the FIRST hidden earthquakes IGNITE under the scan where none were catalogued.
beats.transition: fade up into the moving scan; ignitions spark under it
beats.means: The machine finds what was hidden in the noise.

beats.new: The ignitions multiply across the field; a counter climbs toward about 1,750 as more buried quakes light up.
beats.transition: the scan keeps sweeping; points accumulate + counter ticks
beats.means: The scale of what was hidden — about 1,750.

beats.new: MORPH: the ~1,750 scattered ignitions slide and CONNECT into one glowing crimson line, northwest to southeast.
beats.transition: MORPH — the point-scatter tweens into one continuous line (carry)
beats.means: The pattern is a line, not a cloud.

beats.new: The line settles onto a map of southern Alaska, 250 km long, bone-white station pins along it, label: YAKUTAT MICROPLATE, the razor edge.
beats.transition: the line lands on the map; pins + label disclose
beats.means: This is a real place on Alaska's ground.

beats.new: MAP-TRAVEL into depth: the map tilts and the razor line is revealed as the buried EDGE of a slab diving under Alaska, layered crust above.
beats.transition: MORPH/travel — top-down map rotates into an oblique cross-section (travel)
beats.means: The line is the edge of a subducting plate.

beats.new: The slab grinds; a bright STRESS MARK migrates step by step along the plate boundary as the Yakutat block collides.
beats.transition: a discrete stress mark travels the boundary (a concrete new element, not a glow)
beats.means: The collision loads specific points on the crust.

beats.new: MORPH to a LOW-ANGLE: the ground opens toward a looming second fault to the north; a GHOSTED, dotted PROPOSED stress vector travels north (hypothesis register).
beats.transition: MORPH to a low-angle looming-fault vantage; a dotted vector travels north (travel)
beats.means: A proposed link: stress may reach the Denali Fault.

beats.new: The Denali Fault flares in a controlled, GHOSTED flash; a tag reads 2002 · M7.9 · DENALI with a small 'researchers propose' qualifier.
beats.transition: the fault ghost-flares; the label stamps in, clearly hedged
beats.means: The 2002 quake, framed as hypothesis, never as proven cause.

beats.new: Cool to a clean schematic: the crimson edge locks RAZOR-SHARP on the spatial axis — WHERE the edge sits, known.
beats.transition: everything settles; the edge snaps sharp and certain in space
beats.means: The model knows WHERE — drawn, not captioned.

beats.new: A perpendicular TIME axis extends into the frame and stays FOGGED, refusing to resolve — WHEN it moves, unknown. Small WHERE / WHEN marks support the staging.
beats.transition: the time axis dissolves into fog and will not resolve
beats.means: The honest limit, DRAWN: where yes, when no.

beats.new: The sharp edge pulses once, steady and bright: knowing where it sits sharpens every hazard map and building code.
beats.transition: a calm reaffirming pulse along the edge

beats.new: GRAPHIC-MATCH: the crimson edge line reforms into the ALASKA.AI wordmark rule; the wordmark resolves center.
beats.transition: GRAPHIC-MATCH — the seismic line becomes the wordmark underline (match)
beats.means: Brand sign-off tied to the visual.

beats.new: Tagline reveals: 'what's moving in alaska ai, this week', with a source credit line (Miller et al., ANU · The Seismic Record, 2026).
The last incandescent point drifts along the edge and fades; gentle cinematic fade to black.
beats.transition: fade out, motion running to the final frame
beats.means: A real ending, never a static hold.

shots.centered: the 250 km razor edge on the map
the slab edge diving + the migrating stress mark
a low-angle looming Denali Fault; a ghosted proposed stress vector
the DRAWN limit: edge razor-sharp in space, time axis fogged

ending: Branded outro: the seismic edge line graphic-matches into the ALASKA.AI wordmark rule, tagline + source credit staged after the VO, the last incandescent point drifting and a slow fade. Motion runs to the last frame.
captions: open, voice-synced kinetic phrases from audio/words60.json (CAPTION_SYNC gate); on-screen numbers limited to the validator-verified set (about 1,750; 250 km; M7.9; Yakutat microplate; razor edge; Miller/ANU; The Seismic Record; the 'researchers propose' qualifier on the Denali beat).
voice: edge-tts draft; publish in a config/voices.yaml Kokoro voice
music: freshly sourced per-run, credited (MUSIC gate rejects synth) — taut, low, geologic tension resolving to something clear
